<insights>

- Good cohesion (0.78) confirms these four panels share a common interaction model — they all operate on the analysis/proposal lifecycle, not general CRUD
- The zone includes its host view (analysis.ts) alongside the components, which is appropriate since the view exists solely to compose these panels
- Mutual two-way imports with web-dashboard-application (2 each) are typical for a component zone that both receives shared state and emits events back to the app shell
- Co-locating the analysis view with its component panels is a clean design choice — the view acts as a compositor with no standalone purpose outside this zone.
- smart-add-input and batch-import-panel both have dedicated tests while analyze-panel and proposal-editor do not — consider adding coverage for the proposal editor given its editing responsibilities.
- Low coupling (0.22) despite being a UI zone is a positive signal — the panels are not bleeding concerns into unrelated parts of the codebase.
- The panel components are fully decoupled from both task-usage-analytics and websocket-health-infrastructure — UI input panels and infrastructure services share no import path, confirming clean layering between the UI and server-side concerns
- UI panel zone has no imports from infrastructure zones (task-usage-analytics, websocket-health-infrastructure) — the web package respects a UI/infrastructure split even within the single gravitational hub. This is a positive layering signal that should be preserved.
- proposal-editor.ts has no corresponding test file while all other interactive components in the zone (smart-add-input.ts, batch-import-panel.ts) do. The proposal editor handles user editing of AI-generated content — a high-complexity interaction with no automated regression safety net.
- 'proposal-editor.ts' is semantically an editing/review component for AI-generated proposals, not an input panel — it handles output-review interaction, not data entry. The zone name 'prd-analysis-input-panels' accurately describes analyze-panel, batch-import-panel, and smart-add-input, but misclassifies proposal-editor's interaction model. If proposal-editor grows in complexity, the naming mismatch will make zone membership ambiguous.
- 'proposal-editor.ts' does not match the 'input-panels' naming theme — it is a review/edit component for AI output, not an input panel. Either rename the zone to 'prd-analysis-panels' (dropping 'input') to accommodate the editor, or extract proposal-editor into a sibling zone once it gains the test coverage it currently lacks.
- Zone "panel" was identified from filename patterns, not directory structure — consider creating packages/web/src/viewer/components/prd-tree/panel/ and moving related files there
- [call graph] 160 internal calls, 3 outgoing, 0 incoming (cohesion: 0.98, coupling: 0.02)

</insights>
